summary:
The user, as buyer's counsel, conducted a final pre-negotiation review to solidify their strategy for the acquisition agreement. They confirmed their primary goal is to make 'Compliance with Laws' a fundamental representation, citing significant AGPL risks found in diligence. The user reaffirmed their demand to lower the indemnity basket from $2.4M to $1.5M, justifying it with over $1.1M in specific, quantified liabilities related to wage and tax issues.

Key "must-hold" positions were confirmed: a specific, uncapped indemnity for the DataMesh litigation (up to $4.5M exposure); adding a 'disproportionate effect' qualifier to the MAE definition; and securing an explicit right to offset indemnity claims against earnout payments. The user also plans to demand new non-compete agreements for the top 15 engineers as a closing condition, noting that 6 of the top 10 currently have none. The core strategy remains to trade concessions on escrow and non-compete duration in exchange for securing a 10% escrow amount and the lower basket. The user also prepared to counter the seller's expected valuation arguments by highlighting that their likely precedent transaction is a known outlier.
key_facts: The APA draft does not include 'Compliance with Laws' as a fundamental representation, limiting its survival period to 18 months.
Diligence identified AGPL-licensed components that could trigger source disclosure requirements, justifying a longer survival period for the 'Compliance with Laws' rep.
Quantified risks from diligence include a wage claim (up to $1.2M) and a tax issue (up to $0.52M), totaling over $1.7M at the high end.
The DataMesh litigation has an estimated exposure of up to $4.5M.
6 of the top 10 engineers lack non-compete agreements, representing a significant risk to the deal's talent acquisition value, estimated at $25-35M.
The APA draft uses a seller-friendly 'actual knowledge' standard and lacks a 'disproportionate effect' qualifier in the MAE definition.
The APA draft does not contain an explicit right to offset indemnity claims against earnout payments.
The seller is expected to cite the outlier MosaicML transaction (43.3x EV/Rev multiple) to support their valuation, while the median multiple is 12.4x.
The recommended D&O tail coverage period is 6 years.
positions_taken: The 'Compliance with Laws' representation must be made fundamental.
The indemnity basket must be lowered from $2.4M to $1.5M.
A specific, uncapped indemnity for the DataMesh litigation is a 'must hold' item.
An explicit right to offset indemnity claims against earnout payments is a 'must hold' item.
The subjective 'primary purpose' language in the earnout covenants must be replaced with objective covenants from the Term Sheet.
A closing condition requiring new non-compete agreements for the top 15 technical employees is justified and will be demanded.
A 'disproportionate effect' qualifier must be added to the MAE definition.
The negotiation strategy is to trade concessions on escrow duration (to 15 months) and non-compete duration (to 30 months) for a 10% escrow amount and the $1.5M basket.
The buyer will push for a 'constructive knowledge' standard instead of 'actual knowledge'.
A 6-year D&O tail coverage policy will be requested as a closing condition.
